heading: MongoDB Vector Search Pre-Filtering
body:
The [$vectorSearch](pipe.$vectorSearch) `filter` option matches BSON (Binary Javascript Object Notation)
boolean, date, objectId, numeric, string, and UUID values, including arrays of these types.

You **must** index the fields that you want to filter your data by as the
[index definition](avs-types-vector-search) type in a [index definition](avs-types-vector-search) type index definition. Filtering your data is
useful to narrow the scope of your semantic search and ensure that not
all vectors are considered for comparison.

MongoDB Vector Search supports the [$vectorSearch](pipe.$vectorSearch) `filter` option for
the following MQL (MongoDB Query Language) [operators](manual/reference/mql/query-operators):

| Type      | MQL (MongoDB Query Language) operator |
| --------- | ------------------------------------- |
| Equality  | $eq, $ne                              |
| Range     | $gt, $lt, $gte, $lte                  |
| In set    | $in, $nin                             |
| Existence | $exists                               |
| Logical   | $not, $nor, $and, $or                 |

> **Note:** The [$vectorSearch](pipe.$vectorSearch) `filter` option doesn't support
> other [evaluation query operators](manual/reference/mql/query-predicates),
> [aggregation pipeline operators](manual/reference/mql/expressions), or [MongoDB Search operators](operators-ref).
